definitions.example: Examples of solids include rocks, metals, and ice.
definitions.definition: A state of matter that has a fixed shape and volume. The particles in a solid are closely packed and have a fixed position in space.

definitions.example: Examples of mechanical properties include Young's modulus, bulk modulus, and Poisson's ratio.
definitions.definition: The properties of a solid that describe its response to external forces, such as stress, strain, and elasticity.

definitions.example: Examples of stress include tensile stress, compressive stress, and shear stress.
definitions.definition: A measure of the force applied to a material per unit area, which causes a change in its shape or size.

definitions.example: Examples of strain include tensile strain, compressive strain, and shear strain.
definitions.definition: A measure of the deformation of a material, which is the ratio of the change in length to the original length.

definitions.example: Examples of Young's modulus include steel (200 GPa) and rubber (0.01 GPa).
definitions.definition: A measure of the stiffness of a material, which is the ratio of stress to strain within the proportional limit.

definitions.example: Examples of bulk modulus include steel (160 GPa) and rubber (0.01 GPa).
definitions.definition: A measure of the resistance of a material to volume change under hydrostatic pressure.

definitions.example: Examples of Poisson's ratio include steel (0.3) and rubber (0.5).
definitions.definition: A measure of the lateral strain that occurs when a material is subjected to a longitudinal tensile or compressive load.

definitions.example: Examples of elastic materials include rubber and steel.
definitions.definition: The ability of a material to return to its original shape after the removal of an external force.

definitions.example: Examples of plastic materials include putty and clay.
definitions.definition: The ability of a material to undergo permanent deformation without breaking or shattering.

definitions.example: Examples of Hooke's law include springs and elastic materials.
definitions.definition: A law that states that the force required to stretch or compress a spring is proportional to its displacement from its equilibrium position.

definitions.example: Examples of proportional limit include steel (200 MPa) and rubber (10 MPa).
definitions.definition: The maximum stress or strain that a material can withstand without undergoing plastic deformation.

definitions.example: Examples of elastic limit include steel (200 MPa) and rubber (10 MPa).
definitions.definition: The maximum stress or strain that a material can withstand without undergoing permanent deformation.

definitions.example: Examples of yield point include steel (150 MPa) and rubber (5 MPa).
definitions.definition: The stress or strain at which a material begins to deform plastically.

definitions.example: Examples of ultimate tensile strength include steel (500 MPa) and rubber (10 MPa).
definitions.definition: The maximum stress that a material can withstand before failing or breaking.

definitions.example: Examples of ductile materials include copper and gold.
definitions.definition: The ability of a material to undergo significant plastic deformation before breaking or shattering.

definitions.example: Examples of brittle materials include glass and ceramic.
definitions.definition: The tendency of a material to break or shatter suddenly and without warning when subjected to stress.

definitions.example: Examples of hardness include diamond (9 on Mohs scale) and talc (1 on Mohs scale).
definitions.definition: A measure of the resistance of a material to scratching or abrasion.

definitions.example: Examples of tensile strength include steel (500 MPa) and rubber (10 MPa).
definitions.definition: The maximum stress that a material can withstand in tension before failing or breaking.

definitions.example: Examples of compressive strength include concrete (20 MPa) and brick (10 MPa).
definitions.definition: The maximum stress that a material can withstand in compression before failing or breaking.
